The bioactive constituents of Tussilago farfara include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Coltsfoot is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being dried and used in capsule or powder form to support respiratory health.
